source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of mesenchymal progenitor cells in adipose tissue
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal fibroblast/adipocyte progenitor cells
Fibrillin-1 is an extracellular matrix gene characteristic of adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells
Microfibril-associated glycoprotein 2 is expressed in mesenchymal progenitors and marks adipocyte precursors
CD248 (endosialin) is a marker of mesenchymal stromal/progenitor cells including adipocyte precursors
IGFBP5 is highly expressed in adipose progenitor cells and regulates IGF signaling in adipogenesis
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a hallmark of mesenchymal/fibroblastic adipocyte precursor cells
Decorin is an extracellular matrix proteoglycan characteristic of stromal/fibroblast-like adipocyte precursors
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and a canonical B cell lineage marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a definitive B cell surface marker used clinically for B cell identification
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed in B cells for MHC class II antigen presentation
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is a B cell survival receptor essential for B cell maturation
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR signaling
Immunoglobulin kappa constant region reflects active immunoglobulin production by B cells/plasma cells
J chain is critical for polymeric immunoglobulin assembly in antibody-secreting B cells
CD37 is a tetraspanin predominantly expressed on B cells involved in immune signaling
T cell receptor gamma constant 2 is a defining marker of gamma delta T cells
CD7 is a T/NK cell marker highly expressed on gamma delta T cells
LEF1 is a transcription factor associated with T cell development and naive/memory T cell states
TCF7 is a key transcription factor for T cell identity and marks less differentiated T cells
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor expressed on gamma delta T cell subsets
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor associated with tissue-resident lymphocyte identity
IL7R marks gamma delta T cell subsets and is important for their homeostasis
CD8A is the canonical co-receptor defining CD8+ T cells
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule highly expressed in cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic effector protease in CD8+ cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme H is a cytotoxic serine protease characteristic of effector CD8+ T cells
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic granule-mediated killing by CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule membrane protein highly expressed in effector CD8+ T cells
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated cytotoxic effector lymphocytes
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
CD3G is a T cell receptor complex component confirming T cell lineage

PECAM1 (CD31) is a definitive endothelial cell surface marker

Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule expressed in NK cells
Granzyme K marks less mature/immature NK cell subsets
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory NK receptor characteristic of immature NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) pairs with NKG2A on immature NK cells

L-selectin (CD62L) is expressed on immature/less differentiated NK cells
CMC1 is associated with cytotoxic lymphocyte function in NK cells
TCF7 expression in NK cells marks an immature/less differentiated state
IL-1β is a hallmark pro-inflammatory cytokine of classically activated inflammatory macrophages
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a key chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages for neutrophil recruitment
CXCL2 is an inflammatory chemokine characteristic of activated macrophages

CD163 is a scavenger receptor and macrophage lineage marker
COX-2 (PTGS2) is an inflammatory enzyme upregulated in activated macrophages

Ficolin-1 is a pattern recognition molecule expressed by monocytes/macrophages
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical marker broadly expressed on innate lymphoid cells
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 and broadly marks ILCs
IL-7 receptor is essential for ILC development and homeostasis
KIT (CD117) is expressed on ILC progenitors and ILC subsets
